questions.question: What is the primary purpose of Notion Academy as described in the article?

options.text: To sell Notion templates on a marketplace
options.explanation: Selling templates is one of the lessons, not the overall purpose of the Academy.

options.text: To turn curiosity into capability by teaching Notion and offering badges and certifications
options.explanation: The article states that Notion Academy helps users learn Notion, build workflows, and validate skills with badges & certifications.

options.text: To provide a platform for live webinars only
options.explanation: Webinars are a component, but not the sole purpose of the Academy.

options.text: To replace the Notion Help Center entirely
options.explanation: The Help Center is still mentioned as a resource alongside the Academy.

questions.question: Which learning path is labeled as "Advanced Path" in the article?

options.text: Essentials Path
options.explanation: Essentials Path is for beginners, not advanced learners.

options.text: Workflows Path
options.explanation: Workflows Path is intermediate, not the advanced offering.

options.text: Advanced Path
options.explanation: The article explicitly lists an "Advanced Path" with 5 lessons.

options.text: Custom Agents Path
options.explanation: Custom Agents is a lesson, not a named learning path.

questions.question: How can a learner earn a badge through Notion Academy?

options.text: By completing any single lesson, regardless of difficulty
options.explanation: Badges are tied to completing entire paths, not just one lesson.

options.text: By completing a curated learning path (e.g., Essentials, Workflows, Advanced) at their own pace
options.explanation: The article describes earning badges by finishing a chosen path.

options.text: By attending a live webinar only
options.explanation: Webinars help skill development but badges require path completion.

options.text: By purchasing a Notion subscription plan
options.explanation: Plan purchase is unrelated to badge earning.

questions.question: What is required to become a Notion Certified Admin according to the article?

options.text: Only a free Notion plan is needed
options.explanation: Certification is available for any plan type, but you must complete the admin certification process.

options.text: Completion of the Notion Academy certification program, which covers workspace design, security best practices, and scaling Notion
options.explanation: The article states you get certified to run Notion at scale by completing the certification program.

options.text: Submitting a portfolio of custom templates to the Notion Marketplace
options.explanation: Template creation is a separate lesson, not a certification requirement.

options.text: Attending at least three live webinars
options.explanation: Webinars are optional resources, not a certification prerequisite.

questions.question: Which lesson specifically mentions helping automate repeat work for a team?

options.text: Custom Agents
options.explanation: The "Custom Agents" lesson description says it helps automate repeat work and reduce busywork.

options.text: Sharing & collaboration
options.explanation: That lesson focuses on teamwork, not automation.

options.text: Building basics
options.explanation: This lesson covers fundamentals, not automation.

options.text: Project management
options.explanation: Project management is about structuring tasks, not automating repeat work.

questions.question: What distinguishes the "Essentials Path" from the "Workflows Path"?

options.text: Essentials Path is for beginners and covers basic Notion skills, while Workflows Path is intermediate and focuses on building connected systems with AI.
options.explanation: The article describes Essentials as beginner‑level and Workflows as intermediate with AI assistance.

options.text: Essentials Path includes certification exams, whereas Workflows Path does not.
options.explanation: Certification is a separate offering, not tied to these specific paths.

options.text: Essentials Path requires a paid Notion plan, while Workflows Path is free.
options.explanation: Both paths are available to any plan type.

options.text: Essentials Path focuses solely on template creation, while Workflows Path focuses on project management.
options.explanation: Both paths cover broader skill sets; template creation is just one lesson.

questions.question: If a user wants to build a connected system that unites teams and projects with AI assistance, which path should they choose and why?

options.text: Workflows Path, because it is labeled intermediate and explicitly mentions creating connected systems with AI.
options.explanation: The Workflows Path description states it helps create connected systems that unite teams and projects, with AI assistance.

options.text: Essentials Path, because it provides the foundational knowledge needed before adding AI.
options.explanation: Essentials is beginner‑focused and does not mention AI or advanced workflow integration.

options.text: Advanced Path, because AI features are only covered at the advanced level.
options.explanation: While Advanced covers deeper capabilities, the article specifically ties AI to the Workflows Path.

options.text: Custom Agents lesson, because it automates repeat work, which is equivalent to AI assistance.
options.explanation: Custom Agents focuses on automation but not the broader system‑wide AI‑enabled workflow described.

questions.question: What is the relationship between Notion Academy's live webinars and the certification process?

options.text: Webinars are optional supplemental resources that help learners deepen their knowledge before or after completing certification modules.
options.explanation: The article lists webinars as a way to level up skills, but does not state they are mandatory for certification; they serve as supplemental learning.

options.text: Attending a certain number of webinars is a prerequisite for becoming a Notion Certified Admin.
options.explanation: Certification requirements focus on completing the certification program, not webinar attendance.

options.text: Webinars replace the need for any self‑paced lessons in the certification pathway.
options.explanation: Webinars complement, not replace, the structured lessons and paths.

options.text: Only live webinars grant badges, while recorded lessons do not.
options.explanation: Badges are earned by completing learning paths, not by attending webinars alone.
